title: Consultation: Draft objective for per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances in Canadian drinking water - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation: Draft objective for per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances in Canadian drinking water

## Current status: Closed
Opened on February 11, 2023, and will close to new input on April 12, 2023.
Health Canada has developed a technical document to provide regulatory authorities and decision makers with an objective for per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances in Canadian drinking water supplies. We're looking for comments on this draft document from regulatory authorities, decision makers and the public.

## Join in: How to participate
Send us an email or participate by mail.
Water and Air Quality Bureau
Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON K1A 0K9
Email: water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We invite input from:
- federal, provincial and territorial regulatory and health protection agencies
- industry stakeholders
- academic communities
- non-governmental organizations

## Key questions for discussion
We're looking for comments on these topics:
- the proposed objective
- the approach used to develop it
- the potential impacts of implementation
We'll use the information we gather from this consultation to revise the document as needed.
- Objective for Canadian Drinking Water Quality: Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances
- Water talk: Per-and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) in drinking water
Water and Air Quality Bureau
Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON K1A 0K9
Email: water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca
